---
status: public
shortDescription: "The body of an if-statement executes repeatedly, as long as the condition holds"
shortCorrection: "The body of an if-statement executes at most once"
concepts:
  - Conditional
  - Loop
  - ControlFlow
related:
  # - id: LiteralNoExpression
  #   relation: Special case
inOtherPl:
  - pl: Java
    id: IfIsLoop
  # - pl: Python
  #   id: NoAtomicExpression
spec:
   - id: plr3.8
     sections: ["7.0"]
expressibleInNms:
- id: ControlFlowAsGraph
  how: graph of if statement
plVersion:
  # from: ecma6
  # to: ecma11
references:
  # - citationKey: kaczmarczykIdentifyingStudentMisconceptions2010
  #   id: MMR4
  #   name: "Uninstantiated memory allocation"
  #   relationship: "Students see variable declaration as object allocation"
---
The body of the `if`-statement executes as many times as needed 
to ensure the condition does not hold anymore and execution can continue.

childMdx.rawBody:
- Students claim an `if`-statement means "ensure that this condition holds"
- Students claim an `if`-statement "corrects things whenever this condition does not hold"
Read through Python's tutorial on [Control Flow](https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/controlflow.html) to learn more.

frontmatter.shortCorrection: The body of an if-statement executes at most once
